objectives: Collect and wear all required Personal Protective Equipment correctly
Understand shroud function and its role in preventing steel oxidation during continuous casting
Recognize the importance of timely shroud changes for steel quality and safety
Perform pre-change safety checks by monitoring ladle and tundish status
Operate shroud manipulator arm to safely remove worn shroud
Position and rotate shroud using manipulator controls
Observe and understand lancing operation procedures
Install new shroud and secure it properly at tundish inlet
Coordinate with control room for safe casting resumption
procedures: Collect Personal Protective Equipment: helmet, gloves, boots, jacket, aluminized suit, gold plated face shield, leg guard, skull cap
Observe shroud position and steel flow path from ladle to tundish
Review consequences of worn shroud: reoxidation, flow disruption, breakouts, inclusions
Check digital display board for tonnage remaining in ladle and tundish
Wait for slag detector to detect slag and slide gate to close automatically
Hold shroud manipulator arm from platform
Press down button on manipulator arm
Press clockwise button to rotate shroud
Remove worn shroud from ladle as empty ladle is removed
Observe new ladle arriving at station
Move shroud to the right using manipulator arm before lancing starts
Hold shroud in place during lancing operation performed by NPC coworker
Wait for lancing operation completion confirmation from coworker
Hold shroud manipulator arm to begin installation
Press anti-clockwise button to rotate shroud to upright position
Press up button on manipulator arm to move shroud close to ladle inlet
Press lock button to secure shroud in place
Use walkie-talkie to coordinate with control room for casting resumption
sopContext.constraints: All Personal Protective Equipment must be collected and worn before beginning work. The slide gate must close automatically after slag detector detects slag before shroud change can proceed. User must hold the shroud manipulator arm in place during the entire lancing operation. Shroud must be rotated to correct positions (clockwise for removal, anti-clockwise for installation) using specific buttons. Shroud must be properly locked in place using the lock button before casting resumes. After every four to five heat cycles, the shroud must be changed. In case of damaged or worn out shroud, it must be replaced with a new one and will not be lanced. Casting operations can only resume after coordination with control room via walkie-talkie.
